title: Yadgir Fort
tagline: Yadgir Fort — heritage city of Karnataka

overview.short: Yadgir Fort, also known as Yetagiri is a fortress situated in Yadgir, in the Yadgir District, of the Indian state of Karnataka.
overview.description: Yadgir Fort, also known as Yetagiri is a fortress situated in Yadgir, in the Yadgir District, of the Indian state of Karnataka. The fortress is notable for having been constructed on the top of a 100-meter (328 feet) tall monolith.

topPlaces.name: Yadgir
topPlaces.description: Yadgiri, also spelled as Yādagiri, is a city and the administrative headquarters of Yadgir district in the Indian state of Karnataka. It is also the administrative headquarters of Yadgir Taluku, one of the six taluks of Yadgir.

topPlaces.name: Munderga
topPlaces.description: Munderga is a panchayat village in the southern state of Karnataka, India. Administratively, Munderga is under Yadgir Taluka of Yadgir District in Karnataka.

topPlaces.name: Naikal, Wadgera
topPlaces.description: Naikal is a village in the southern state of Karnataka, India. It lies at the foot of a rocky hill called Parmanan, along the Yadgir-Shahapur Road (State Highway 15 (Karnataka)).

topPlaces.name: Hattikuni
topPlaces.description: Hattikuni is a panchayat village in the southern state of Karnataka, India. It is located in the Yadgir taluk of Yadgir district in Karnataka.

topPlaces.name: Thangunda
topPlaces.description: Thangunda is a panchayat village in the southern state of Karnataka, India. Administratively, Thangunda is under Yadgir Taluka of Yadgir District in Karnataka.

items.note: Yadgiri, also spelled as Yādagiri, is a city and the administrative headquarters of Yadgir district in the Indian state of Karnataka. It is also the administrative headquarters of Yadgir Taluku, one of the six taluks of Yadgir.
Munderga is a panchayat village in the southern state of Karnataka, India. Administratively, Munderga is under Yadgir Taluka of Yadgir District in Karnataka.
Naikal is a village in the southern state of Karnataka, India. It lies at the foot of a rocky hill called Parmanan, along the Yadgir-Shahapur Road (State Highway 15 (Karnataka)).
Hattikuni is a panchayat village in the southern state of Karnataka, India. It is located in the Yadgir taluk of Yadgir district in Karnataka.
Thangunda is a panchayat village in the southern state of Karnataka, India. Administratively, Thangunda is under Yadgir Taluka of Yadgir District in Karnataka.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Yadgir Fort?
faq.a: The best time to visit Yadgir Fort is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Yadgir Fort?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Rajiv Gandhi International Airport, Hyderabad (147 km) and the nearest railway station is Kacheguda (Hyderabad) (160 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Yadgir Fort?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Yadgir Fort at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Yadgir Fort?
faq.a: Stays in Yadgir Fort start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Yadgir Fort?
faq.a: The top attractions are Yadgir, Munderga, Naikal, Wadgera, Hattikuni, Thangunda.
